memory.summary: The leisure option feels tempting, but the term project deadline looms too close to ignore. I should prioritize the quick chore first to clear mental space before tackling the looming work.
That trivial chore yielded almost no progress, confirming my instinct to skip it for the high-impact term work. I will redirect that energy immediately toward the project deadline.
Skipping the trivial chore was right; that hour on the project directly buys me breathing room later. I must keep this momentum going before the afternoon slips away entirely.

The 2-hour sprint pushed the term project to 45%, validating that direct focus on high-impact tasks yields immediate breathing room. I must preserve this momentum before fatigue compromises my remaining energy.
The 1.5-hour sprint advanced the project by 9%, confirming that direct focus on high-impact tasks yields immediate breathing room before fatigue sets in.
That final hour cemented the project at 64%, proving my deliberate focus on high-impact work is the only reliable path to future breathing room, even as fatigue mounts.
My deliberate focus on the high-impact term project successfully advanced progress, yet my avoidance of the trivial chore resulted in a missed instant win as energy depleted.
The 2-hour sprint advanced the project to 77%, validating that deliberate focus on high-impact work secures future breathing room despite mounting fatigue.
Pushing through exhaustion to hit 85% validates my deliberate strategy: securing academic outcomes now is the only way to guarantee future breathing room, even at the cost of immediate comfort.
Reaching 93% validates that my deliberate investment in the term project secures future safety, even as my energy reserves hit zero.
Finishing the project at 100% validates my deliberate strategy, securing academic safety despite hitting absolute energy zero. I feel a hollow sense of relief rather than satisfaction.
Spending energy on trivial chores feels like a betrayal of my deliberate strategy, yet the instant win offers a fleeting distraction from my hollow exhaustion.
